content: Just as human language learners use lexical and conceptual reflection to solidify new vocabulary and correct usage errors, large language models can integrate the graded reflection framework of Reflexion into the iterative self-feedback loop of Self-Refine to capture incremental, context-aware improvements that single-stage self-feedback misses. This hierarchical reflection-refinement structure addresses a key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine, which often overlooks subtle, long-horizon flaws in output that require persistent memory of past iterations to identify.
